meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve can't finish projects (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't finish projects. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Project Template to Solve Shipping
content.seo_description: Stop stalling your SaaS. Use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to bypass the 'can't finish' trap and ship your MVP with speed and precision.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' trap stems from excessive friction in scaffolding and architectural decision fatigue. By deploying an Antigravity project template, you automate the boilerplate, while Claude Code enforces iterative delivery. This combination forces momentum, ensuring your MVP remains focused on core value rather than getting lost in endless configuration.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: the initial spark of a SaaS idea, a few hours of frantic coding, and then the slow, painful decay into 'feature creep' and technical paralysis. Your GitHub is a graveyard of half-baked repos, and the market is moving while your project sits in a state of perpetual incompletion.

It isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of velocity. The Antigravity project template is the antidote to this inertia, providing a high-octane launchpad that handles the plumbing so you can focus on the profit-generating core of your application. Let’s stop building toys and start shipping products.

steps.title: Define the Value-First Scope
steps.description: Before writing code, define the singular problem your MVP solves to prevent scope bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business idea: [Insert Idea]. Create a lean technical specification focusing only on the core value proposition and a 7-day MVP roadmap.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review this technical spec and identify any features that are not strictly necessary for an MVP.'

steps.title: Initialize with Antigravity
steps.description: Use the template to generate an opinionated, production-ready directory structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the 'SaaS-Starter' template with authentication, database ORM, and Stripe integration pre-configured.

steps.title: Establish the Data Schema
steps.description: Define your database models to ensure the core logic is grounded in reality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS app that tracks user subscriptions and project status. Use Prisma syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the schema to the database and generate the migration files.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Feature
steps.description: Focus strictly on the one mechanic that makes your product worth paying for.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer function that performs [Core Action]. Ensure it includes robust error handling and logging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the core service logic in the backend and expose it via a secure API endpoint.'

steps.title: Automated Testing & Deployment
steps.description: Ensure the product is stable before moving to production environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate comprehensive unit tests for the core service layer using Jest.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run all tests and provide a summary of any failures or coverage gaps.'

pro_tips: Treat every feature request as a cost-benefit analysis; if it doesn't directly contribute to ARR, move it to the 'V2' backlog.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you go, ensuring technical debt is minimized from day one.
Ship the UI without custom branding first; focus on utility before aesthetics to validate the market demand.
Automate your deployment pipeline using GitHub Actions to remove the mental friction of 'pushing to production'.
Set a strict 30-day deadline for your MVP launch to force prioritization of the 'must-haves' over 'nice-to-haves'.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a staging environment, run a smoke test on your primary user flow, and share the link with 5 potential customers for immediate feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the methodology to finally break the cycle of unfinished projects and launch a revenue-generating SaaS. Don't let another idea die in a local repo. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
